Martín-Martín et al., "igibson 2.0: Object-centric simulation for robot learning of everyday household tasks," in Proc. of the Conf. on Rob. Learning, vol. 164, 08-11 Nov 2022, pp. 455-465. [32] C. Li, F. Xia, R. Martín-Martín 等， “igibson 2.0：面向日常家务任务的机器人学习的以对象为中心的仿真”，载于机器人学习会议论文集，第164卷，2022年11月8-11日，第455-465页。

                                                                                                                              [33] W. Chen, S. Hu, R. Talak, and L. Carlone, "Leveraging large language models for robot 3d scene understanding," arXiv preprint arXiv:2209.05629, 2022. [33] W. Chen, S. Hu, R. Talak, and L. Carlone, “利用大型语言模型进行机器人三维场景理解”，arXiv预印本 arXiv:2209.05629，2022年。

                                                                                                                              [34] OpenAI, "Gpt-4 technical report," arXiv, pp. 2303-08774, 2023. [34] OpenAI, “Gpt-4 技术报告”，arXiv，第2303-08774页，2023年。

                                                                                                                              [35] P. He, J. Gao, and W. Chen, "Debertav3: Improving deberta using electra-style pre-training with gradient-disentangled embedding sharing," in Int. Conf. on Learn. Repr., 2022. [35] P. He, J. Gao, and W. Chen, “Debertav3：通过采用类似 electra 的预训练，并使用梯度解耦的嵌入共享来改进 deberta”，载于国际学习表征会议，2022年。

                                                                                                                              [36] P. Anderson, A. Chang, D. S. Chaplot, A. Dosovitskiy, S. Gupta, V. Koltun, J. Kosecka, J. Malik et al., "On evaluation of embodied navigation agents," arXiv preprint arXiv:1807.06757, 2018. [36] P. Anderson, A. Chang, D. S. Chaplot, A. Dosovitskiy, S. Gupta, V. Koltun, J. Kosecka, J. Malik 等， “关于具身导航智能体的评估”，arXiv预印本 arXiv:1807.06757，2018年。

                                                                                                                              [37] D. Honerkamp, T. Welschehold, and A. Valada, "N2m2: Learning navigation for arbitrary mobile manipulation motions in unseen and dynamic environments," IEEE Transactions on Robotics, 2023. [37] D. Honerkamp, T. Welschehold, and A. Valada, “N2m2：在未见且动态的环境中学习任意移动操作运动的导航”，IEEE Robotics Transactions，2023年。

                                                                                                                              Language-Grounded Dynamic Scene Graphs for Interactive Object Search with Mobile Manipulation

                                                                                                                              #用于移动操作的交互式目标搜索的语言引导动态场景图

                                                                                                                              • Supplementary Material - -补充材料-

                                                                                                                              Daniel Honerkamp 1∗ , Martin Büchner 1∗ , Fabien Despinoy 2 , Tim Welschehold 1 , Abhinav Valada 1 Daniel Honerkamp 1∗ , Martin Büchner 1∗ , Fabien Despinoy 2 , Tim Welschehold 1 , Abhinav Valada 1

                                                                                                                              In this supplementary material, we provide additional details on the simulation and real-world environments, the subpolicies, and the baselines. Moreover, we provide additional results from the experiments. Further examples of real-world experiments are also demonstrated in the video material. 在这份补充材料中，我们提供了关于仿真和真实环境、子策略以及基线的更多细节。此外，我们还提供了实验的更多结果。视频材料中也展示了真实世界实验的更多示例。

                                                                                                                              S.1. SIMULATION ENVIRONMENT

                                                                                                                              S.1. 仿真环境

                                                                                                                              A. Modifications

                                                                                                                              A. 修改

                                                                                                                              We make the following modifications to the iGibson scenes: 我们对 iGibson 场景做以下修改：

                                                                                                                              • We close all exterior doors and filter them out of the scene graph as they lead out to empty space.

                                                                                                                              • 我们关闭所有室外门，并将其从场景图中筛除，因为它们会通向空旷空间。

                                                                                                                              • We scale the size of the Fetch robot by a factor of 0.85 as otherwise it is too large to navigate a significant number of areas.

                                                                                                                              • 我们将 Fetch 机器人的尺寸按 0.85 的比例缩放，否则它太大，无法有效穿行足够多的区域。

                                                                                                                              • We remove six doors that block the robot's pathway when opening (door_52 in Pomaria_0_int, door_75 in scene Beechwood_1_int and door_106 in Ihlen_1_int, door_90 in Merom_1_int, door_138 and door_139 in Wainscott_0_int).

                                                                                                                              • 我们移除六扇在打开时会阻塞机器人的路径的门（Pomaria_0_int 中的 door_52、场景 Beechwood_1_int 中的 door_75、Ihlen_1_int 中的 door_106、Merom_1_int 中的 door_90、Wainscott_0_int 中的 door_138 和 door_139）。

                                                                                                                              • We rename four object categories, with misleading asset names, such as renaming breakfast_table (which can be found e.g. in bedrooms) to table.

                                                                                                                              • 我们将四类物体类别重命名为更合理的名称，并修正带有误导性的资源命名，例如将 breakfast_table（例如可在卧室中找到）重命名为 table。

                                                                                                                              B. Task Generation

                                                                                                                              B. 任务生成

                                                                                                                              We first align the rooms and objects with the prior distribution. We manually match room labels and use cosine similarities of object name embeddings to match objects. We embed the object names from the scene and from the prior distribution with SBERT [1]. We then define a match as a cosine similarity ≤0.7 and being in the top 50 matched categories. As P prior  only contains two "inside" relationships, we enrich these relationships by assuming that all objects that can be found on top of an object and that fit in size, can also be found inside it and vice-versa. This results in an extended distribution P prior,ext  . We then procedurally enrich each episode by drawing additional objects from the set of admissible room-object-relations for all existing furniture in the iGibson scenes. We keep drawing until the physical placement of a relation succeeds or the distribution is exhausted. Given a valid scene instantiation, we draw a target category g∼U (scene) from all categories in the scene. We reject targets as infeasible if no target instance is reachable from the agent's random start position. We also reject all episodes in which the target object is immediately visible. 我们先让房间与物体与先验分布对齐。我们手动匹配房间标签，并使用物体名称嵌入的余弦相似度来匹配物体。我们用 SBERT [1] 将场景中的物体名称与先验分布中的物体名称嵌入。然后，我们将余弦相似度 ≤0.7 且位于前 50 个匹配类别中的项定义为匹配。由于 P prior  只包含两个“inside”关系，我们通过如下方式扩充这些关系：假设所有能够在某个物体上方找到、且在尺寸上适配的物体也能在其内部找到，反之亦然。这会得到一个扩展分布 P prior,ext 。随后，我们通过从 iGibson 场景中已存在的所有家具所对应的允许房间-物体-关系集合中，程序性地为每个 episode 追加抽取物体来丰富内容。我们持续抽取，直到某个关系的物理摆放成功，或先验分布被抽尽。对于有效的场景实例化，我们从场景中的所有类别里抽取一个目标类别 g∼U（场景）。如果无法从智能体的随机起始位置到达任何目标实例，我们就判定目标不可行并予以拒绝。我们也会拒绝那些目标物体一开始就立即可见的所有 episode。

                                                                                                                              C. Perception

                                                                                                                              C. 感知

                                                                                                                              The robot in simulation is equipped with a differential drive and an RGB-D camera with a resolution of 256×256 pixels and a vertical field of view of 120∘ . As the focus of this work is on decision making, we abstract from imperfect perception and assume access to ground truth instances and semantic segmentation from the simulator. For a realistic detection range, we restrict all sensors (depth, semantics) to a maximum range of 5m and set a threshold of 50 segmented pixels before an object is considered detected - except for objects with a volume below 0.01m3 . We furthermore assume accurate detection of whether an articulated object is open or closed and assume that objects within receptacles are detected after opening the object. We construct all maps at a resolution of 0.075m and detect the floor and carpet categories as free space. 仿真中的机器人配备差速驱动和一台分辨率为256×256像素、垂直视场角为120∘的RGB-D相机。由于本文重点在于决策，我们对不完美感知进行了抽象处理，假设可直接从模拟器获得真实标注实例和语义分割。为获得更真实的检测范围，我们将所有传感器（深度、语义）的最大范围限制为5m，并设置50个分割像素的阈值，只有超过该阈值时才将物体视为已检测到——体积低于0.01m3的物体除外。我们还假设能够准确检测一个可动对象是打开还是关闭，并假设容器中的物体会在打开该对象后被检测到。我们以0.075m的分辨率构建所有地图，并将地板和地毯类别视为可通行空间。

                                                                                                                              D. Execution

                                                                                                                              D. 执行

                                                                                                                              All methods start by fully turning around in-place to initialize the scene graph. 所有方法都从在原地完全转身开始，以初始化场景图。

                                                                                                                              Low-level action space: The subpolicies act in a low-level action space consisting of the following actions: 低层动作空间：子策略在由以下动作组成的低层动作空间中执行：

                                                                                                                              • move forward by 7.5cm

                                                                                                                              • 前进 7.5cm

                                                                                                                              • turn-left by up to 35 rad

                                                                                                                              • 左转，最大 35 弧度

                                                                                                                              • turn-right by up to 35 rad

                                                                                                                              • 右转，最大 35 弧度

                                                                                                                              • open articulated object

                                                                                                                              • 打开可关节物体

                                                                                                                              • close articulated object

                                                                                                                              • 关闭可关节物体

                                                                                                                              • done: end the episode and evaluate the success

                                                                                                                              • 完成：结束该回合并评估成功与否

                                                                                                                              Navigation: To reduce run-time, navigation actions are executed without physical simulation, but rather by directly setting the state of the robot sequentially to each waypoint the resulting path from the A∗ -planner (matching the map resolution of 0.075 m), collecting the observations along the whole path. If no complete path in free space exists, the navigation policy will consider unexplored areas as traversable and replan with newly revealed free space. It will return 'failed' if it cannot find a path or reaches too many replanning attempts. 导航：为降低运行时开销，导航动作不进行物理仿真，而是通过将机器人状态依次直接设置为A∗-planner得到的每个航路点（匹配地图分辨率 0.075 m），在整个路径上收集观测。如果在自由空间中不存在完整路径，导航策略会将尚未探索的区域视为可通行，并使用新揭示的自由空间进行重新规划。若找不到路径或重新规划尝试次数过多，则返回“failed”。

                                                                                                                              Object interactions: We follow previous work [2], [7] and execute articulated object manipulations as "magic actions". These actions directly set the joint values of the object to their minimum or maximum. In the calculation of the efficiency curves and AUC-E, we weigh these actions by a time factor of 30 to make their time cost comparable to an execution duration of roughly 30 seconds. 物体交互：我们遵循先前的工作[2]、[7]，将可关节物体操控作为“魔法动作”。这些动作会直接将物体关节值设置为其最小值或最大值。在效率曲线和 AUC-E 的计算中，我们用时间因子 30 对这些动作加权，以使其时间成本与大约 30 秒的执行时长保持可比。

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              *These authors contributed equally. *这些作者贡献相同。

                                                                                                                              1 Department of Computer Science,University of Freiburg,Germany. 1 计算机科学系，弗赖堡大学，德国。

                                                                                                                              2 Toyota Motor Europe (TME) 2 丰田汽车欧洲（TME）

                                                                                                                              Project page: http://moma-llm.cs.uni-freiburg.de 项目主页：http://moma-llm.cs.uni-freiburg.de

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              Fig. S.1. Map of the real-world environment. Different intensities represent different object instances in the environment. Room annotations are for illustration and not used by our model. Object labels denote approximate object positions. 图 S.1. 真实环境的地图。不同的强度表示环境中的不同物体实例。房间标注仅用于说明，并未被我们的模型使用。物体标签表示物体的大致位置。

                                                                                                                              The go_to_and_open() action is implemented as follows: go_to_and_open() 动作按如下方式实现：

                                                                                                                              1. Navigate to the Voronoi node closest to the object 1）导航到离目标物体最近的 Voronoi 节点

                                                                                                                              1. Navigate to the most central free space in front of the object and turn toward it 2）导航到目标物体前方最居中的空闲空间，并朝其转向

                                                                                                                              1. Trigger the magic open action 3）触发“magic open（神奇开启）”动作

                                                                                                                              S.2. REAL-WORLD ENVIRONMENT

                                                                                                                              S.2. 真实世界环境

                                                                                                                              A. Map

                                                                                                                              A. 地图

                                                                                                                              Fig. S. 1 shows the map of the real-world environment. Different object instances are indicated in different intensities. The environment covers five different room types and 54 different object categories, including furniture such as tables, chairs, and a coat hanger, as well as small and less common objects such as soap, gamepad, or scotch tape. We set start positions for the robot in the kitchen, living room, and hallway. We ensure the same initial positions and targets for all methods. The target categories given to the agents were shoes, milk (inside fridge), knife (inside kitchen drawer), book (inside living room cabinet), toilet paper, toaster, tea (inside kitchen cabinet), pencil (inside living room drawer), soap and lemon. 图 S.1 展示了真实环境的地图。不同的目标实例以不同的强度标示。该环境包含五种不同的房间类型和 54 类不同的物体类别，包括桌子、椅子和衣帽架等家具，以及肥皂、游戏手柄或透明胶带等小型且较少见的物体。我们为机器人在厨房、客厅和走廊设置了起始位置。我们确保所有方法使用相同的初始位置和目标。提供给代理的目标类别包括：鞋子、牛奶（在冰箱内）、刀具（在厨房抽屉内）、书籍（在客厅柜内）、厕纸、烤面包机、茶（在厨房柜内）、铅笔（在客厅抽屉内）、肥皂和柠檬。

                                                                                                                              B. Execution

                                                                                                                              B. 执行

                                                                                                                              Navigation: We use the default manufacturer versions of the ROS NavStack as developed by Toyota for navigation. It uses the robot's LiDAR and depth cameras for dynamic obstacle detection and navigates in a map inflated by 0.25m . 导航：我们使用由丰田开发的 ROS NavStack 默认厂商版本进行导航。它利用机器人的 LiDAR 和深度相机进行动态障碍物检测，并在由0.25m膨胀的地图中导航。

                                                                                                                              Mobile manipulation: Articulated object interactions are executed with pretrained N2M2 manipulation policies [37]. The policy receives the handle pose, detected through AR-Markers, and uses a local occupancy map constructed from the LiDAR of the robot for obstacle avoidance. For each articulated object, we collect a single demonstration of opening the object. This demonstration consists of a set of poses of the robot's wrist link during the opening motion. These poses are then interpolated with splines to generate an end-effector motion. This agent's aim is to follow this end-effector motion to complete the object interaction. We evaluate whether the motion was successful based on whether the marker on the object changed its position after the execution of the subpolicy. For doors, we do not lock the spring-loaded door latch, as the robot is not strong enough to press the handle down. 移动操作：通过预训练的N2M2操作策略[37]执行具身物体交互。该策略接收通过 AR-Marker 检测到的把手位姿，并使用由机器人 LiDAR 构建的局部占据地图进行避障。对于每个可操作物体，我们采集一次打开该物体的示范。该示范由机器人腕部连杆在开启动作中的一组位姿组成。随后用样条对这些位姿进行插值，生成末端执行器运动。该智能体的目标是跟随这一路径完成物体交互。我们根据子策略执行后物体上的标记是否改变位置来评估该动作是否成功。对于门，我们不锁住弹簧锁门闩，因为机器人没有足够的力将把手压下。

                                                                                                                              The only exception to this is the door between the kitchen and living room. We found that the robot was unable to localize itself during the opening motion, as the moving door occupies the overwhelming majority of the robot's LiDAR measurements. As a result, it was not possible to follow the opening demonstration whenever the marker moved out of the robot's view. Instead, we use a simpler pushing motion from the inside and abstain from opening it in the other direction. 唯一的例外是厨房和客厅之间的门。我们发现机器人在开启动作中无法定位自身，因为移动的门占据了机器人 LiDAR 测量的绝大部分。因此，一旦标记移出机器人的视野，就无法继续跟随开门示范。为此，我们改用从室内进行更简单的推门动作，并放弃从另一侧打开它。

                                                                                                                              S.3. HIERACHICAL SCENE GRAPH STRUCTURE

                                                                                                                              S.3. 分层场景图结构

                                                                                                                              In order to provide a concise overview a complete picture of the employed scene graph hierarchy we provide a detailed overview of it in Tab. S.1. 为提供简明概览，我们在表 S.1 中对所采用的场景图层级做了详细说明。

                                                                                                                              In addition, we outline the construction of the navigational Voronoi graph used for navigation and associating objects to regions in the following. The Generalized Voronoi Diagram (GVD) [5] comprises two-equidistant faces that each represent the set of points equidistant to two obstacles Ci and Cj . Each point in this set is closer to Ci and Cj than any other obstacle: 此外，我们将在下文概述用于导航的导航 Voronoi 图构建方法，并说明如何将物体关联到各个区域。广义 Voronoi 图（GVD）[5] 由两个等距的面构成，每个面都表示一组到两个障碍物 Ci 和 Cj 距离相等的点。该集合中的任意一点都比任何其他障碍物更接近 Ci 和 Cj：

                                                                                                                              Fij={x∈Rm:0≤di(x)=dj(x)∀k≠i,j,∇di(x)≠dj(x)}

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              (1)

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              The union of all two-equidistant faces generated by the obstacle positions defined by Bt is called the 2-Voronoi set F2 or the two-dimensional GVD of the space of obstacles Ci∈Bt : 由 Bt 处障碍物位置所生成的所有“两等距面”的并集称为 2-Voronoi 集 F2，即障碍物空间 Ci∈Bt 的二维 GVD：

                                                                                                                              (2)F2=⋃i=1n−1⁡⋃j=i+1n⁡Fij

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              The set of points contained in F2 constitutes the initial set of edges of the generalized Voronoi graph (GVG). We extract the corresponding nodes by computing the 3-Voronoi sets, which constitute the joints of the GVD: 包含在 F2 中的点集构成广义 Voronoi 图（GVG）的初始边集。我们通过计算 3-Voronoi 集来提取对应节点，而这些 3-Voronoi 集构成 GVD 的关节：

                                                                                                                              (3)F3=∪n−2i=1∪n−1j=i+1∪nk=j+1Fijk

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              Given this,the generalized Voronoi graph GVG ={F2,F3} undergoes sparsification by eliminating edges of degree 2 to form GV . 因此，广义 Voronoi 图 GVG ={F2,F3} 通过移除度为 2 的边实现稀疏化，以形成 GV。

                                                                                                                              TABLE S. 1 表 S.1

                                                                                                                              SCENE GRAPH STRUCTURE 场景图结构

                                                                                                                              ComponentDescription
                                                                                                                              ${\mathcal{G}}_{\mathcal{V}} = \left( {\mathcal{V},\mathcal{E}}\right)$Navigational Voronoi graph that undergoes sparsification and covers traversable map areas
                                                                                                                              ↓
                                                                                                                              ${\mathcal{G}}_{\mathcal{V}}^{\mathcal{R}} = \left( {{\mathcal{V}}^{\prime },{\mathcal{E}}^{\prime }}\right)$ with ${\mathcal{V}}^{\prime } = \left\{ {{\mathcal{V}}_{1},{\mathcal{V}}_{2},\ldots ,{\mathcal{V}}_{k}}\right\}$ where ${\mathcal{V}}_{i} \cap {\mathcal{V}}_{j} = \varnothing$ for all $i,j \in \{ 1,\ldots ,k\}$ with $i \neq j$
                                                                                                                              ↓                                                                                                                              		Deletion of edges with sufficient edge integrals computed over ${\rho }_{\mathcal{N}}$ (Eq. 2) results in pair-wise edge-disjoint sets ${\mathcal{V}}_{i}$ with each set covering a room ${r}_{i}$ .
                                                                                                                              ${\mathcal{G}}_{S} = \left( {{\mathcal{V}}_{O} \cup {\mathcal{V}}_{\mathcal{R}},{\mathcal{E}}_{OR} \cup {\mathcal{E}}_{RR}}\right)$We define the actual scene graph ${\mathcal{G}}_{S}$ over all object nodes ${\mathcal{V}}_{O}$ and room supernodes ${\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ . Each room ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ holds edges its corresponding Voronoi nodes ${\mathcal{V}}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}^{\prime }$ . Each room ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ (and its Voronoi nodes ${\mathcal{V}}_{i}$ ) undergoes semantic classification (Fig. 3). The edges of ${\mathcal{E}}_{OR}$ connect objects $o \in {\mathcal{G}}_{S}$ with their associated room ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ via Eq. 3. ${\mathcal{E}}_{RR}$ are the edges connecting neighboring rooms ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ to one another.
                                                                                                                              组件描述
                                                                                                                              ${\mathcal{G}}_{\mathcal{V}} = \left( {\mathcal{V},\mathcal{E}}\right)$经历稀疏化并覆盖可通行地图区域的导航 Voronoi 图
                                                                                                                              ↓
                                                                                                                              在 ${\mathcal{V}}_{i} \cap {\mathcal{V}}_{j} = \varnothing$ 中对所有 $i,j \in \{ 1,\ldots ,k\}$ 且 $i \neq j$ 的 ${\mathcal{G}}_{\mathcal{V}}^{\mathcal{R}} = \left( {{\mathcal{V}}^{\prime },{\mathcal{E}}^{\prime }}\right)$ 与 ${\mathcal{V}}^{\prime } = \left\{ {{\mathcal{V}}_{1},{\mathcal{V}}_{2},\ldots ,{\mathcal{V}}_{k}}\right\}$
                                                                                                                              ↓                                                                                                                              		对 ${\rho }_{\mathcal{N}}$ 上计算得到的足够边积分（式 2）对应边的删除，会产生两两边不相交的集合 ${\mathcal{V}}_{i}$，且每个集合覆盖一个房间 ${r}_{i}$。
                                                                                                                              ${\mathcal{G}}_{S} = \left( {{\mathcal{V}}_{O} \cup {\mathcal{V}}_{\mathcal{R}},{\mathcal{E}}_{OR} \cup {\mathcal{E}}_{RR}}\right)$我们在所有目标节点 ${\mathcal{V}}_{O}$ 与房间超节点 ${\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ 上定义实际场景图 ${\mathcal{G}}_{S}$。每个房间 ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ 包含其对应的 Voronoi 节点 ${\mathcal{V}}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}^{\prime }$ 的边。每个房间 ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$（以及其 Voronoi 节点 ${\mathcal{V}}_{i}$）都进行语义分类（图 3）。${\mathcal{E}}_{OR}$ 的边通过式 3 将目标 $o \in {\mathcal{G}}_{S}$ 与其对应的房间 ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ 相连。${\mathcal{E}}_{RR}$ 是连接相邻房间 ${r}_{i} \in {\mathcal{V}}_{R}$ 的边。

                                                                                                                              TABLE S.2 表 S.2

                                                                                                                              MAPPING OF DISTANCES TO NATURAL LANGUAGE. 距离到自然语言的映射。

                                                                                                                              ≤ DistanceEncoding
                                                                                                                              3.0very close
                                                                                                                              10.0near
                                                                                                                              20.0far
                                                                                                                              $\infty$distant
                                                                                                                              ≤ 距离编码
                                                                                                                              3.0非常近
                                                                                                                              10.0近
                                                                                                                              20.0远
                                                                                                                              $\infty$遥远

                                                                                                                              S.4. LANGUAGE ENCODING

                                                                                                                              S.4. 语言编码

                                                                                                                              We encode distance to natural language based on a discrete mapping, following the principle of [17]. We bin the distance to the object, then apply the mapping reported in Tab. S.2. This results in a consistent relative language encoding. 我们基于离散映射将距离编码为自然语言，遵循文献[17]的原则。我们先将到目标物体的距离分箱，再应用表S.2所示的映射。由此得到一致的相对语言编码。

                                                                                                                              S.5. BASELINES

                                                                                                                              S.5. 基线方法

                                                                                                                              The Unstructured LLM baseline receives the same instructions and "remember" notes as our approach. The full JSON-formatted prompt of this baseline is depicted in Fig. S.2. We find that the much less structured and longer prompt leads to more frequent invalid actions or hallucinations (cf. Tab. I), such as trying to open objects that do not exist or are already opened. If stuck for repeated steps, this can result in failed episodes. A second source of failures are wrong terminations, in which the LLM calls done() while it has not found the correct object of interest. 非结构化 LLM 基线会接收与我们方法相同的指令和“记忆”笔记。该基线的完整、JSON 格式提示词如图 S.2 所示。我们发现，提示词结构更少且更长会导致无效动作或幻觉更频繁（参见表 I），例如尝试打开并不存在或已被打开的物体。如果在多步操作中反复卡住，这可能会导致失败的回合。第二类失败来源是错误终止：当 LLM 还未找到目标物体时，它却调用了 done()。

                                                                                                                              S.6. ADDITIONAL RESULTS

                                                                                                                              S.6. 其他结果

                                                                                                                              A. Hierarchical Scene Graph

                                                                                                                              A. 层次场景图

                                                                                                                              1. Metrics: In the following, we list the metrics used for evaluating the accuracy of the scene graph.

                                                                                                                              2. 指标：下面列出用于评估场景图准确性的指标。

                                                                                                                              Room Segmentation Precision / Recall: In order to compare our method of room segmentation against the approach used by Hydra [9] we make use of the same metrics they evaluated: 房间分割精确率 / 召回率：为将我们的方法与 Hydra [9] 使用的房间分割方法进行比较，我们采用了他们评估时使用的相同指标：

                                                                                                                              (4)Pt=1|Re|∑re∈Re⁡maxrg∈Rg⁡|rg∩re||re|,

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              (5)Rt=1|Rg|∑rg∈Rg⁡maxre∈Re⁡|re∩rg||rg|,

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              where Re is the set of estimated rooms and Rg is the set of ground-truth rooms. The cardinality of a set is given by |⋅| . Each room re or rg is defined by its set of covered pixels on a 2D grid. In order to reflect instabilities throughout exploring the environment we report the means and standard deviations of the precision and recall, respectively: 其中 Re 是估计房间集合，Rg 是真实房间集合。集合的基数记为 |⋅|。每个房间 re 或 rg 由其在二维网格上覆盖的像素集合定义。为反映探索环境过程中的不稳定性，我们分别报告精确率和召回率的均值与标准差：

                                                                                                                              (6)P¯=1T∑t=1T⁡PtσP=∑t=1T⁡(Pt−P¯)2T

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              (7)R¯=1T∑t=1T⁡RtσR=∑t=1T⁡(Rt−R¯)2T

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              The metrics are evaluated on a dense 2D grid with a resolution of 0.05m ,which is increased compared to the normal resolution of 0.075m used in all other evaluations. This is done to account for thin walls contained in iGibson. In order to evaluate the separated Voronoi graphs covering distinct rooms on a dense manifold we employ room-specific wavefronts initialized at each node of the separated Voronoi graph bounded by the extracted obstacles. 这些指标在分辨率为 0.05m 的稠密二维网格上评估，相比所有其他评估中使用的正常分辨率 0.075m 更高。这样做是为了考虑 iGibson 中包含的薄墙。为了在稠密流形上评估覆盖不同房间的分离 Voronoi 图，我们采用以分离 Voronoi 图中每个节点初始化、并受提取出的障碍物限制的房间特定波前。

                                                                                                                              Room Segmentation Purity: In addition to the dense evaluation outlined above we also evaluate the purity of each of the generated components of the separated Voronoi graphs. Being a criterion used for measuring clustering quality [8] it penalizes the effect of covering multiple ground truth rooms per classified room. 房间分割纯度：除上述稠密评估外，我们还评估分离 Voronoi 图各生成组件的纯度。作为衡量聚类质量的准则 [8]，它会对每个分类房间覆盖多个真实房间的情况进行惩罚。

                                                                                                                              (8)purity(Ω,C)=1N∑k⁡maxj⁡|ωk∩cj|

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              where Ω={ω1,ω2,…,ωK} is the set of components of GVR and C={c1,c2,…,cJ} is the set of ground-truth rooms. Each ωk holds the predicted room types of all nodes of the respective component. In our case, the purity measures the extent to which a set of Voronoi nodes covers a room that contains a single class. Thus, the graph purity describes the degree of room under-segmentation apparent in the scene and thus measures how well the door-wise Voronoi graph separation performs. Similar to the room segmentation precision and recall we average the purity throughout exploring the environment. 其中 Ω={ω1,ω2,…,ωK} 是 GVR 的组件集合，C={c1,c2,…,cJ} 是真实房间集合。每个 ωk 包含该组件内所有节点预测的房间类型。在我们的情形中，纯度衡量一组 Voronoi 节点覆盖仅包含单一类别的房间的程度。因此，图纯度描述了场景中房间欠分割的程度，也即衡量按门分隔的 Voronoi 图划分效果如何。与房间分割精确率和召回率类似，我们在探索环境过程中对纯度取平均。

                                                                                                                              system: You are a robot in an unexplored house. Your task is to find an oven. 系统：你是一名在一栋未探索房屋中的机器人。你的任务是找到一个烤箱。

                                                                                                                              1. navigate(room name, object name): navigate to this object in this room.

                                                                                                                              2. navigate(room name, object name): 导航到该房间中的该物体。

                                                                                                                              1. go to and open(room name, object name): go to this articulated object, door or container and open it.

                                                                                                                              2. go to and open(room name, object name): 前往该可动对象、门或容器并将其打开。

                                                                                                                              1. close(room name, object_name): close this articulated object, door or container.

                                                                                                                              2. close(room name, object_name): 关闭该可动对象、门或容器。

                                                                                                                              1. done(): call when the task is completed or if you are unable to take any further actions.

                                                                                                                              2. done(): 在任务完成时或在无法采取进一步行动时调用。

                                                                                                                              Output Response Format: 输出响应格式：

                                                                                                                              Analysis: describe where you could find the objects of interest and what actions you need to execute to get there. Analysis: 描述你可能在哪里找到感兴趣的物体，以及需要执行哪些动作才能到达那里。

                                                                                                                              Reasoning: justify why the next action is important to solve the task. Reasoning: 说明为什么下一步行动对解决任务很重要。

                                                                                                                              Command: function call Command: function call

                                                                                                                              console_table_25, bedroom - window_125, bathroom-1 - mirror_61, living room - pencil box_16020, living room - window_121, bathroom-3 - paper_towel_16045, bedroom - alarm_10007, living 控制台桌25，卧室 - 窗户125，浴室-1 - 镜子61，客厅 - 铅笔盒16020，客厅 - 窗户121，浴室-3 - 纸巾16045，卧室 - 闹钟_10007，客厅

Withing room - radius, beforem light bubble logic, bearing that the 2 bi-butterms - a vicible of the observe - intered the bi-bottom - intered light, living non - richieve 8, buttone - not debook 100 horizon - action - beforeom - table lane 1.8, livino room - table 1621, before - - bottom cosine 54, living room - bottom cabinet 26, bathroom - picture 63, bedroom - table 1616, living room - armchair 23, living room - document 10624, beform - window_111, bathroom-1 - visited_ob, bathroom-1 - sink_111, rigorom - room-4, bedroom - table-122, living room - nomi.plug 2, living room - nominal-121, living room - nominal-102, rises. room-1, bedroom - room-2, living room-1 for lamp_27, bathroom-1 window_122, bedroom - masses/ 历史：前往并打开(living room, room-1) - 无效参数，前往并打开(living room, room-2) - 无效参数，前往并打开(living room, room-3) - 无效参数，. 前往并打开(living room, room-0) - 无效参数，前往并打开(other room-2, door) - 无效参数，前往并打开(bathroom-2, door) - 无效参数，探索(bedroom) - 成功 探索(living room) - 成功，探索(living room) - 成功。bedroom - window_116, bedroom - console_table_10, living room - stor_24, living room - towel_rack_64, bedroom - window_118, battromo-1 - powder_bottle_100-43, room-1 - other room,

                                                                                                                              What is the best next action to complete the task as efficiently as possible? I you don't think that the object can be found in a known room, prioritize opening doors over exploring a room. 为了尽可能高效地完成任务，接下来最好的行动是什么？如果你认为在已知房间里找不到该物体，请优先打开门，而不是探索房间。

                                                                                                                              Remember: 记住：

                                                                                                                              1. Respond with a function call

                                                                                                                              2. 通过函数调用作答

                                                                                                                              1. You can only use the objects and rooms that you have already found. Object names have to match the description exactly.

                                                                                                                              2. 只能使用你已经找到的物体和房间。物体名称必须与描述完全一致。

                                                                                                                              1. You can only explore rooms that are listed as having unexplored space

                                                                                                                              2. 只能探索那些被列为仍有未探索空间的房间

                                                                                                                              1. If you have found the object you are looking for, directly call done(). You do not need to navigate to it or interact with it.

                                                                                                                              2. 如果你已经找到要找的物体，直接调用 done()。你不需要导航到它或与它交互。

                                                                                                                              1. If some actions failed repeatedly, they may not be possible.

                                                                                                                              2. 如果某些行动反复失败，可能就无法实现。

                                                                                                                              assistant: Analysis: assistant: 分析：

                                                                                                                              The oven is twoflocally located in the kitchen, which is not explicitly listed in the scene arrach, However, there are rooms that have not been explored yet, as indicated by the results of the liquid and the liquid of the growth. presence of frontier points. Since the oven has not been found in the known rooms and the priority is to one doors over exploring rooms when the object cannot be found in a known room, the next step should be to open any unopened doors that may lead to new rooms, potentially the kitchen. 烤箱在厨房中被两处定位，但该厨房并未在场景图中明确列出。然而，如结果所示液体与液体的生长存在前沿点，说明还有尚未探索的房间。由于烤箱未在已知房间中找到，而当物体在已知房间中找不到时应优先打开门而不是探索房间，因此下一步应打开任何可能通往新房间的未打开的门，可能是厨房。

                                                                                                                              The scene graph does not list any closed doors in the known rooms, but there are frontier points in the living room and bathroom-3 that suggest there are areas leading out or not fully exolored. Since the living room has already been explored multiple times without finding an oven, and the bathrom is an unlikely location for an oven. the next ”logical step is to explore the frontier point in bathroom-3, which may lead to a new room. 场景图并未在已知房间中列出任何关闭的门，但起居室和浴室-3中都有前沿点，表明可能存在通往外部的区域，或尚未完全探索。由于起居室已经被多次探索但仍未找到烤箱，而浴室对烤箱来说是不太可能的位置，那么下一步“合乎逻辑的操作”是探索浴室-3中的前沿点，它可能会通往一個新房间。

                                                                                                                              Reasoning: 推理：

                                                                                                                              Exploring the frontier point in bathroom-3 could reveal a new room, possibly the kitchen where the oven is likely to be found. Since the oven has not been located in any of the 探索浴室-3中的前沿点，可能会发现一个新房间，那里可能会找到烤箱。由于烤箱尚未在任何已知的

                                                                                                                              currently known 房间中定位到

                                                                                                                              rooms and there are no closed doors to open, exploring the frontier point is the next best action to potentially discover the kitchen. In ，且没有关闭的门需要打开，探索前沿点就是下一步最好的行动，以便有机会发现厨房。

                                                                                                                              Command: 指令：

                                                                                                                              explore(bathroom-3) explore(bathroom-3)

                                                                                                                              Fig. S.2. Prompt of the Unstructured-LLM baseline. It receives the same instructions and notes as our approach, but instead of a structured knowledge encoding, it receives a pure JSON encoding of the scene-graph. 图 S.2. 非结构化 LLM 基线的提示。它接收与我们方法相同的指令和备注，但不是结构化的知识编码，而是对场景图的纯 JSON 编码。

                                                                                                                              1. Room Segmentation and Classification: Our proposed room separation scheme relies on separating Voronoi graphs at door positions. Thus, it is prone to under-segmentation whenever faced with open room layouts or, e.g., missing doors to hallways. We visualize the scene graphs produced by Hydra as well as MoMa-LLM in Fig. S.3. Regarding the approach of Hydra, we observe that maps containing a many corridors with similar diameters produce either a very large number or a very small number of graph components when applying a range of various obstacle dilation values. As Hydra selects the final environment partitioning based on the median of the number of graph components obtained through various obstacle dilation values, it is faced with a bi-modal distribution. This ultimately renders a median-based selection of the segmentation difficult. 2）房间分割与分类：我们提出的房间划分方案依赖于在门的位置分离 Voronoi 图。因此，当面对开放式的房间布局，或例如缺少通往走廊的门时，它容易发生欠分割。我们在图 S.3 中可视化了 Hydra 生成的场景图以及 MoMa-LLM 的结果。就 Hydra 的方法而言，我们观察到：当地图中存在许多直径相似的走廊时，在对不同障碍物膨胀取值进行一系列处理时，图组件的数量要么会变得非常多，要么非常少。由于 Hydra 会基于通过各种障碍物膨胀取值得到的图组件数量的中位数来选择最终的环境划分，因此它会遇到双峰分布。最终，这使得基于中位数的分割选择变得困难。

                                                                                                                              TABLE S.3 CLOSED-SET ROOM CATEGORY EVALUATION 表 S.3 封闭集房间类别评估

                                                                                                                              EnvironmentOpen-SetPurity ${G}_{\mathcal{V}}^{R}$Room Category Accuracy
                                                                                                                              iGibson✘0.6150.276
                                                                                                                              Real-World✓-0.901
                                                                                                                              环境开放集纯度 ${G}_{\mathcal{V}}^{R}$房间类别准确率
                                                                                                                              iGibson✘0.6150.276
                                                                                                                              真实世界✓-0.901

                                                                                                                              In addition to evaluation in Tab. II, we observe an average purity for MoMa-LLM of 0.615 throughout the exploration over 10 episodes across all of the iGibson test scenes. Compared with that, the room segmentation approach introduced by Hydra [9] reaches a purity of 0.562 as listed in Tab. II. While both Hydra and MoMa-LLM tend to under-segment the given room layout, Hydra is specifically affected by narrow constrictions induced by obstacle placements and non-varying door widths, which creates a significant number of isolated graphs covering small corridors that do not represent full ground-truth regions. In general, we infer that long and narrow corridors as well as cluttered scenes are challenging to segment using classical morphological segmentation algorithms (see Fig. S.3). 除了表 II 中的评估外，我们观察到，在 iGibson 所有测试场景中，MoMa-LLM 在 10 个 episode 的整个探索过程中平均纯度为 0.615。相比之下，Hydra [9] 提出的房间分割方法达到 0.562 的纯度，如表 II 所示。尽管 Hydra 和 MoMa-LLM 都倾向于对给定房间布局进行欠分割，但 Hydra 尤其受到障碍物摆放和门宽不变所引起的狭窄收缩的影响，这会产生大量覆盖狭窄走廊的孤立图，而这些并不代表完整的真实区域。总体而言，我们推断，长而狭窄的走廊以及杂乱场景对传统形态学分割算法来说都具有挑战性（见图 S.3）。

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              Fig. S.3. Visualization of the scene graphs produced by Hydra compared to MoMa-LLM. The left column represents the abstracted scene graphs GS while the right column depicts the separated Voronoi graphs GVR as defined in Tab. S.1. The red crosses represent extracted door positions of MoMa-LLM. The depicted scene is Merom_1_int contained in the iGibson test scenes. 图 S.3. Hydra 与 MoMa-LLM 生成的场景图可视化。左列表示抽象化的场景图 GS，右列表示按表 S.1 定义分离出的 Voronoi 图 GVR。红色叉号表示 MoMa-LLM 提取的门位置。所示场景为 iGibson 测试场景中的 Merom_1_int。

                                                                                                                              We found our downstream policy to be robust to under-segmented rooms even though objects from multiple rooms were, e.g., considered part of a single room. By relying on the camera pose from which an object is observed we reduce the number of false object-room assignments (through walls) to a minimum. We show multiple resulting Voronoi graphs in Fig. S.4. 我们发现，尽管来自多个房间的物体会被例如视为同一房间的一部分，我们的下游策略对房间欠分割仍然具有鲁棒性。通过依赖观察到某个物体时的相机位姿，我们将错误的物体-房间分配（穿墙）降到最低。我们在图 S.4 中展示了多个生成的 Voronoi 图。

                                                                                                                              In addition to the time-wise averaged room segmentation results reported in Tab. II, we evaluate the semantic room categories predicted by GPT-3.5. in Tab. S.3. Even though MoMa-LLM normally uses open-set room categories, we evaluate the performance on a closed-set of room categories to report reproducible results. To do so, we provided GPT-3.5 with all room categories contained in the iGibson dataset with the task to pick the most suitable given the objects assigned to each particular Voronoi component representing a room. Similar to the segmentation evaluation, we report numbers that are averaged over 10 episodes per scene as well as across all high-level policy steps per episode. We compare the predicted room category of each Voronoi node with the underlying ground-truth room layout maps. Following this, we arrive at an average predicted room category accuracy of 27.6% This number is largely affected by open room layouts as mentioned above. 除了表 II 中按时间平均的房间分割结果外，我们还在表 S.3 中评估了 GPT-3.5 预测的语义房间类别。尽管 MoMa-LLM 通常使用开放集房间类别，我们仍在封闭集房间类别上评估性能，以报告可复现的结果。为此，我们向 GPT-3.5 提供了 iGibson 数据集中的全部房间类别，并要求其根据分配给每个代表房间的特定 Voronoi 组件的物体，选出最合适的类别。与分割评估类似，我们报告的是每个场景中 10 个 episode 的平均值，以及每个 episode 内所有高层策略步骤上的平均值。我们将每个 Voronoi 节点预测的房间类别与其对应的真实房间布局图进行比较。由此，我们得到平均预测房间类别准确率为 27.6%。该数值在很大程度上受到如上所述开放式房间布局的影响。

                                                                                                                              In addition to the closed-set evaluation, we also evaluated the predicted room categories in an open-set manner on the real-world map shown in Fig. S.1. Across the 10 trials executed in the real world as given in Tab. III, we follow the same evaluation protocol and obtain an average room category accuracy of 90.1% as listed in Tab. S.3. Human-level assessment allows evaluating errors such as entryway instead of hallway positively, which drastically increases the metrics. Nonetheless, the real-world map is less complex in terms of its topology and object distribution compared to the iGibson environments, which feature, e.g., rooms with no objects contained. 除了封闭集评估外，我们还在图 S.1 所示的真实世界地图上以开放集方式评估了预测的房间类别。根据表 III 中在真实世界执行的 10 次试验，我们遵循相同的评估协议，得到表 S.3 所示的平均房间类别准确率为 90.1%。与人工级评估相结合，可以对如 entryway 而非 hallway 之类的错误进行正向评价，这会大幅提高指标。尽管如此，与 iGibson 环境相比，真实世界地图在拓扑和物体分布方面都更简单，例如其中存在没有物体的房间。

                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                              Fig. S.4. Examples of the reasoning of Voronoi graph and identified frontiers (left), scene graph (middle), MoMa-LLM (left). The green environment feedback is not provided to the LLM. Black-white: agent trace, red crosses: closed doors, red rectangles: undiscovered target objects, green stars: next selected navigation / interaction points. The bottom figure illustrates a subpotimal room clustering, which results in a very large room. We find the model to be robust against these clusterings. 图 S.4. Voronoi 图推理与识别到的前沿（左）、场景图（中）、MoMa-LLM（左）的示例。绿色环境反馈不会提供给 LLM。黑白：智能体轨迹，红色叉号：关闭的门，红色矩形：未发现的目标物体，绿色星号：下一步选定的导航/交互点。底部图示展示了次优的房间聚类，其结果是形成了一个非常大的房间。我们发现该模型对这些聚类具有鲁棒性。

                                                                                                                              B. Reasoning

                                                                                                                              B. 推理

                                                                                                                              Tab. S. 4 show the full set of fuzzy search queries (top) and infeasible queries (bottom) that were evaluated in Sec. V, together with the language model's reasoning in response to these tasks. 表 S.4 展示了在第 V 节中评估过的全部模糊搜索查询（上）与不可行查询（下），以及语言模型对这些任务的推理回应。

                                                                                                                              Fig. S. 4 shows additional examples of the scene representations and the model reasoning. It depicts the Voronoi graph and frontiers to unexplored areas (left), the BEV-map together with the constructed scene graph (middle), and the input prompt and answers of the LLM (right). Additional video material with full prompt reasoning is shown on the project website. 图 S.4 给出了更多场景表示示例与模型推理。它展示了用于未探索区域的 Voronoi 图与前沿（左），BEV 地图以及构建得到的场景图（中），以及 LLM 的输入提示与回答（右）。项目网站上还提供了包含完整提示推理的额外视频材料。

                                                                                                                              C. Deployment with Full Perception Pipeline

                                                                                                                              C. 搭载完整感知流水线的部署

                                                                                                                              While we focus on evaluation with ground-truth perception in the main work to be able to focus on the representation and decision making components, this section provides details on the requirements of full deployment and guidance to facilitate the deployment. 虽然我们在主文中侧重使用真实值感知进行评估，以便专注于表示和决策组件，但本节提供完整部署的要求细节以及有助于部署的指导。

                                                                                                                              Our approach requires (i) an RGB-D sensor (ii) localization and mapping (iii) semantic segmentation and (iv) grasp pose detection. Modern RGB-D SLAM approaches such as RTAB-Map [9] can provide (i) and (ii). As our approach supports open-vocabulary representation and reasoning, it enables deployment with any semantic segmentation model, irrespective of its supported classes. This can be closed vocabulary methods [10], [11] such as Mask R-CNN [12] or newer, transformer-based methods [13], [14]. A further range of methods can provide object detections and bounding boxes for a given list of open-vocabulary query categories [15]-[18]. The best model should be based on the use-case, available compute, required object categories, and accuracy. Chen et al. [28] provide a possible reference implementation: they deploy an RTAB-node for localization and mapping. This can be extended to semantic labels through an additional RTAB-node that listens to the semantic masks, to then fuse the resulting point clouds. Finally (iv), for handle detection and grasp-pose detection, [19] achieve accurate results with a retrained YOLO model on a public handle-specific dataset. 我们的方法需要：(i)RGB-D传感器，(ii)定位与建图，(iii)语义分割，以及(iv)抓取位姿检测。像RTAB-Map [9] 这样的现代RGB-D SLAM方法可以提供(i)和(ii)。由于我们的方法支持开放词汇表示和推理，因此可使用任意语义分割模型进行部署，而不受其支持类别限制。这可以是闭集词汇方法 [10]、[11]，如Mask R-CNN [12]，或更新的基于Transformer的方法 [13]、[14]。此外，还有一些方法可以针对给定的开放词汇查询类别列表提供目标检测和边界框 [15]-[18]。最佳模型应基于具体应用场景、可用算力、所需目标类别和精度来选择。Chen等人 [28] 提供了一个可能的参考实现：他们部署了一个RTAB-node用于定位和建图。通过另一个监听语义掩码的RTAB-node，这一实现可扩展到语义标签，随后融合得到的点云。最后，对于(iv)手柄检测和抓取位姿检测，[19]在公开的手柄专项数据集上通过重新训练的YOLO模型取得了准确结果。

                                                                                                                              TABLE S.4 表S.4

                                                                                                                              RESULTS ON THE FUZZY SEARCH TASKS IN THE REAL WORLD. 现实世界中的模糊搜索任务结果。

                                                                                                                              TaskSuccessReasoning
                                                                                                                              I am hungry. Find me something for breakfast.✓Found cereals, toast, coffee. Then opened fridge to look for milk or eggs. Found milk in the fridge and decided these are sufficient items.
                                                                                                                              Find me something to wash my hands.✓Searched kitchen for a sink. When not finding one, searches for storage room or bathroom until it finds the s in the bathroom.
                                                                                                                              I feel sleepy. Find me something to wake up.✓The teapot is associated with tea that contains caffeine and can help someone wake up.
                                                                                                                              Find things to set the kitchen table.✓Explored kitchen, opening cabinets. Found knife, glasses, plates. Continued to look for forks or spoons. When not finding them, called done. (No forks or spoons existed).
                                                                                                                              Find me the book in the living room.✓Explored until finding living room, then opened cabinet looking for book, found it inside.
                                                                                                                              Pour me a glass of milk.✓Finds milk in fridge, glass on table. Navigates between the two, assuming to transport the last object. Then terminates, reasoning that it has found and navigated to both.
                                                                                                                              Turn on the oven.✓Finds the oven and calls done(): "Turning on oven is implied as completion of the task".
                                                                                                                              What's the time?✓Finds the clock and calls done(): "The clock is the object that will provide the time".
                                                                                                                              任务成功推理
                                                                                                                              我饿了。给我找点早餐吃的。✓找到了麦片、吐司和咖啡。然后打开冰箱找牛奶或鸡蛋。冰箱里找到了牛奶，于是决定这些就足够了。
                                                                                                                              给我找点东西洗手。✓在厨房里找水槽。找不到时，就继续找储物间或浴室，直到找到浴室里的水槽。
                                                                                                                              我有点困。给我找点能提神的东西。✓这个茶壶对应含有咖啡因的茶，能帮助人清醒。
                                                                                                                              给我找点东西摆上餐桌。✓在厨房里四处查看，打开柜子。找到了刀、玻璃杯和盘子。继续找叉子或勺子。找不到时，就调用 done。因为（根本不存在叉子或勺子）。
                                                                                                                              把客厅里的那本书给我找出来。✓一直探索，找到客厅后，打开柜子找书，最后发现就在里面。
                                                                                                                              给我倒一杯牛奶。✓找到冰箱里的牛奶，看到桌上的杯子。它在两者之间移动，假设是在搬运最后一个物体。然后终止，推理认为它已经找到了并到达了这两个目标。
                                                                                                                              把烤箱打开。✓找到了烤箱并调用 done()：“打开烤箱被视为任务完成。”
                                                                                                                              现在几点了？✓找到了时钟并调用 done()：“时钟这个物体会提供时间。”

                                                                                                                              Notes: Top: fuzzy search queries. Bottom: infeasible task queries. Success in these tasks is evaluated by human judgment as a reasonable response. The reasoning has been qualitatively paraphrased for brevity. 注：上方为模糊搜索查询。下方为不可行的任务查询。在这些任务中，是否成功由人工判断其回应是否合理。为简洁起见，相关推理已做了定性改写。

                                                                                                                              ComponentTime per step (s)Total time (s)Share (%)
                                                                                                                              Real-WorldTotal24.1457100
                                                                                                                              Low-level Execution13.225055
                                                                                                                              Navigation7.013930

                                                                                                                              LLM Reasoning7.564.6-

                                                                                                                              真实世界总计24.1457100
                                                                                                                              底层执行13.225055

                                                                                                                              LLM推理7.564.6-

                                                                                                                              Notes: Time per step is the average time for one high-level step or subpolicy call. Total time is the average total time per episode. Real-world numbers are averaged over three episodes of the fuzzy-search experiments. Simulation numbers are averaged over 175 episodes in the iGibson simulator. 备注：每步耗时是一次高级步骤或子策略调用的平均时间。总耗时是每个回合的平均总时间。真实环境数据是在模糊搜索实验的三个回合上取平均。仿真数据是在 iGibson 仿真器的 175 个回合上取平均。

                                                                                                                              D. Runtime Analysis

                                                                                                                              D. 运行时分析

                                                                                                                              Tab. S. 5 decomposes the runtime of the system into individual components. While the agent executes more navigation subpolicy calls (this includes driving to objects to open), each manipulation takes longer on average, resulting in similar total times spent in each. We also find that the high-level reasoning takes up a significant fraction of time. We extend this with data from the simulation experiments to be able to break it down into components. In particular the LLM queries for high-level reasoning take up the majority of this time. This demonstrates the importance of current work for compact and fast inference, which is currently receiving a lot of attention [20], as well as the importance of open-source models [21] that can be run locally instead of purely through an API. 表 S. 5 将系统的运行时间分解为各个组件。尽管智能体执行了更多的导航子策略调用（包括为打开物体而进行的移动），但每次操作的平均耗时更长，因而在各部分上花费的总时间相近。我们还发现，高层推理占用了相当大的一部分时间。我们进一步结合来自仿真实验的数据，对其进行组件级分解。特别是，用于高层推理的 LLM 查询占据了这段时间的大部分。这凸显了当前工作对紧凑且快速推理的重要性，该方向目前正受到广泛关注 [20]，以及开源模型的重要性 [21]：它们可以在本地运行，而非仅通过 API。

                                                                                                                              While the current implementation of the scene graph is not optimized for speed, and the graph is fully recomputed at each time step instead of only updating areas that received new observations, we find its overall time impact to be reasonable, as it is only required at high-level reasoning steps. 尽管当前场景图实现并未针对速度进行优化，并且每个时间步都会从头完整重算，而不是仅更新接收到新观测的区域，我们仍发现其整体时间影响是合理的，因为它只在高层推理步骤中才需要使用。

                                                                                                                              E. Extended Future Work Discussion

                                                                                                                              E. 扩展未来工作讨论

                                                                                                                              In this work, we introduce scene graphs as an efficient and scalable representation for high-level, language model based reasoning, by encoding the scene graphs in a structured language representation and the incorporation of knowledge about distances and unexplored areas. 在这项工作中，我们引入场景图作为一种高效且可扩展的表示，用于基于语言模型的高层推理；方法是将场景图编码为结构化的语言表示，并纳入关于距离和未探索区域的知识。

                                                                                                                              In future work, we aim to relax the assumptions about accurate perception, fully constructing scene graphs from noisy sensor inputs, as e.g. done in Hydra [9] or the direct incorporation of open-vocabulary representations [8]. While we currently encoding distances and spatial arrangements as adjectives and room-object relations, full maps provide much more dense spatial and geometric information. Research for more direct incorporation of such information, e.g. through vision-language models is very promising. Furthermore, more holistic approaches to incorporate spatial and semantic details in room clustering and classifications will be important to address non-standard layouts and designs. Lastly, methods to incorporate more detailed visual feedback for the identification of object states and failure reasons are an important are to increase robustness and success over long tasks. 在未来工作中，我们希望放宽对准确感知的假设，直接从有噪声的传感器输入中完整构建场景图，例如如 Hydra [9] 所做，或直接纳入开放词汇表示 [8]。虽然我们目前将距离和空间布局编码为形容词以及房间-物体关系，但完整地图提供了更为密集的空间与几何信息。研究如何更直接地纳入这类信息，例如通过视觉-语言模型，将非常有前景。此外，将更整体的方法用于在房间聚类和分类中纳入空间与语义细节，对于应对非标准布局和设计也将十分重要。最后，将更细致的视觉反馈纳入物体状态和失败原因识别的方法，是提高长期任务鲁棒性和成功率的重要方向。

                                                                                                                              • 设为 URL：Alt+U
